pi_items.source: setup_task.sh generates ToO protocol and GCN alert specifically for GRB 221009A.
verifier.py strictly enforces GRB_RA = 19.2176, which accurately matches the synthetic GCN Notice RA of 288.264 degrees.
verifier.py strictly enforces GRB_DEC = 19.7733, which accurately matches the synthetic GCN Notice DEC of +19.7733 degrees.
verifier.py explicitly defines and enforces COORD_TOL_ARCMIN = 15.0.
too_protocol.txt defines Slot 1 as Luminance, and verifier.py checks for filter slot 1 or Luminance/Clear in header.
too_protocol.txt explicitly requires 10 seconds exposure, which is checked by verifier.py logic.
too_protocol.txt explicitly requires 30 seconds exposure, which is checked by verifier.py logic.
too_protocol.txt requires >= 3 images for confirmation phase.
too_protocol.txt requires >= 10 images for science phase.
setup_task.sh creates this specific path and export_result.sh precisely targets it for scanning.
setup_task.sh ToO protocol instructs saving the report to this path, and export_result.sh extracts data from it.
verifier.py explicitly includes Occupation: Observatory Operator in its docstring.
The task focuses entirely on a Gamma-Ray Burst Target-of-Opportunity follow-up, which directly aligns with Time-Domain Astrophysics.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated GCN notices using real-world coordinates for GRB 221009A (RA 19.2176h, Dec 19.7733 deg). The verifier and setup scripts confirm that the agent must execute a ToO protocol requiring a pointing tolerance of 15 arcmin and utilizing Filter Slot 1 (Luminance). The protocol mandates a minimum of 3 confirmation frames at 10 seconds exposure and 10 science frames at 30 seconds exposure. The agent must save images to /home/ga/Images/grb/221009A/ and write a report to /home/ga/Documents/gcn_circular.txt.
